info.title: Platform API
info.description:
API Documentation # API overview The API uses the HTTPS protocol, following the REST principles (POST, GET, DELETE, PUT requests). The API endpoints accept and return data in the JSON format, with the corresponding "application/json" content type header. # Authentication and security The Platform API is only accessible using HTTPS, and all API endpoints require authentication with an API key. # Usage limits Rate limits determine how frequently you can call a particular endpoint. Usually, our platform have different tiers (for example, ESSENTIAL,PROFESSIONAL,ANALYTICS) corresponding to different capabilities or rate limits with the API. # Versioning The API supports versioning of its endpoints through a version suffix in the endpoint URL. This suffix has the following format: "vX", where X is the version number. For example: v2/device. All requests must include the version suffix in the endpoint URL. Any new backwards-incompatible change will be released in a new version. Following changes are not considered to break compatibility: - adding extra fields to returned objects - adding optional fields to input objects # Paging Some API requests will support paging for return a list of data. The items will be retrieved via multiple requests. The paging section in the response will specify the next index for the next request. # Errors Our platform uses conventional HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request. Codes in the 2xx range indicate success. Codes in the 4xx range indicate an error that failed given the information provided (e.g. Don't have privilege to access this device, etc.). Often the response will also include a message explaining the error.
API Changelist # API Changelist ## 6.0.1 Release date: March 04, 2026 ** Account ** - Added GET /API/v2/loginInfo endpoint for retrieving login information and rendering preferences - Returns user preferences, permissions, and features when authenticated via Bearer token - Returns organisation defaults, API key permissions, and features when authenticated via API key ** Process Definitions ** - Added GET /API/v2/processes endpoint for retrieving a paginated list of process definitions - Added GET /API/v2/processes/{id} endpoint for retrieving a process definition by id - Added POST /API/v2/processes endpoint for creating a new process definition with full descriptor (steps, edges, guards) - Added PUT /API/v2/processes/{id} endpoint for updating an existing process definition - Added DELETE /API/v2/processes/{id} endpoint for deleting a process definition - All process definition endpoints require `process_manager` feature and `PROCESS_VIEW` or `PROCESS_MANAGE` permissions ** Device Process States ** - Added GET /API/v2/devices/{serial}/processStates endpoint for retrieving current process states for a device - Added PUT /API/v2/devices/{serial}/processStates/{processId} endpoint for assigning a device to a specific process state - Added POST /API/v2/processes/{id}/assignState endpoint for bulk assigning multiple devices to a process state - Device process state endpoints require `process_manager` feature and `PROCESS_VIEW` or `PROCESS_MANAGE` permissions ** Device ** - Added `lastBeaconSerial` field (String) to device metadata (TrackerMetadata) containing the serial of the beacon that positioned the device for its last location - Added `lastBeaconRssi` field (Integer) to device metadata (TrackerMetadata) containing the RSSI signal strength from the beacon that positioned the device - Added `lastBeaconName` field (String) to device metadata (TrackerMetadata) containing the name of the beacon that positioned the device for its last location - These fields are only populated when the device's last location was determined by a beacon scan - Added `currentFloor` field (Integer) to TrackerMetadata for multi-floor indoor location tracking - The field represents the floor level where the device was last located (0 = ground floor, positive values for upper floors, negative values for basement levels) - Floor is determined by the infrastructure device (geobeacon/zone anchor) with the strongest signal during BLE processing ** Partner MDF (Market Development Fund) ** - Added GET /API/v2/partners/{partnerId}/mdf endpoint for retrieving all MDF records for a partner - Added POST /API/v2/partners/{partnerId}/mdf/calculate endpoint for calculating MDF for a given quarter - Added POST /API/v2/partners/mdf/payment endpoint for recording a payment against MDF credit (partnerId and mdfId in request body) - Added PUT /API/v2/partners/mdf/payment/{paymentId} endpoint for updating a payment (amount in request body) - Added DELETE /API/v2/partners/mdf/payment/{paymentId} endpoint for deleting a payment - Added POST /API/v2/partners/mdf/recalculate endpoint for recalculating MDF for current quarter across all partners - Added POST /API/v2/partners/mdf/recalculate-all endpoint for recalculating MDF for all quarters across all partners - All MDF endpoints require PARTNER_MANAGE permission ** Geozone visits ** - Added POST /API/v2/geozonevisits/byFilter endpoint for retrieving paged geozone visits across multiple devices. Request body (JSON): - `from` (required): ISO-8601 start date/time - `to` (required): ISO-8601 end date/time (max 366 days window) - `maxResults` (optional, default 1000, max 10000): Maximum results per page - `startIndex` (optional, default 0): Pagination offset - `deviceFilter` (optional): Device filter object (same format as POST /devices/byFilter). Supports types: TAG (by asset tag names), STRING (by identifier), ORGANISATION (by org ID), MULTI (combine filters) - `geozoneFilter` (optional): Geozone filter object (same format as POST /geozones/byFilter). Supports types: TAG (by geozone tag names), STRING (by identifier), MULTI (combine filters) Response: {count, data, success} where data contains GeozoneVisitExternalDto items. Time window overlap rule: returns visits where entryTime <= to AND (exitTime > from OR exitTime IS NULL). Requires geozone_visit_report feature (PROFESSIONAL and higher) and GEOZONE_VISIT_VIEW permission. ** Location ** - Added `floor` field (Integer) to AggregatedDataLocationDto for location history endpoints - Floor values match the TrackerMetadata currentFloor field - Added `messageTimeAvailable` field (Boolean) to location and activity data endpoints - When true, the timestamp was provided by the device; when false, the server receive time was used ## 6.0.0 Release date: January 27, 2026 ** Device ** - Added `structuredAddress` field to device metadata (TrackerMetadata) containing structured address components: - `city`: City name (e.g., "Brussels") - `region`: Region/state/province name (e.g., "Vlaams Gewest") - `countryCode`: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., "BE") - `countryName`: Full country name (e.g., "Belgium") - `postalCode`: Postal/ZIP code (e.g., "1000") - `fullAddress`: Complete address string (backwards compatible with existing `lastAddress` field) - Structured address data is available in all device endpoints that return TrackerMetadata: - GET /API/v2/devices/{serial} - GET /API/v2/devices - POST /API/v2/devices/search - Webhook payloads ** Location ** - Added `structuredAddress` field to AggregatedDataLocationDto containing the same structured address components as device metadata - Structured address is resolved via reverse geocoding when location data is processed - Addresses derived from geozone names are excluded from automatic reverse geocoding - Added `altitude` field (Integer, in meters) to AggregatedDataLocationDto for location data endpoints ** Webhook ** - Added `altitude` field (Integer, in meters) to location and activity webhook payloads - Altitude is included when available from GPS-based location sources ** Organisation ** - Added `currency` field to GET /API/v2/organisations/{id} response containing the organization's currency code (e.g., "EUR", "USD") ## 5.6.22 Release date: December 24, 2025 ** Device ** - Added POST /API/v2/devices/search endpoint for searching devices by key identifiers. Returns full device metadata (TrackerMetadata) like other device endpoints. ** Webhook ** - Added optional `name` field to webhook creation and update requests in POST /API/v2/webhooks and PUT /API/v2/webhooks/{id} - Added `name` field to webhook responses in GET /API/v2/webhooks and GET /API/v2/webhooks/{id} - When no name is provided, a descriptive default name is automatically generated based on data types, protocol, and monitoring target: - Device tracking: `location-http-devices`, `activity-battery-status-http-devices` - Alert management: `alert-http-alerts`, `alert-clear-mqtt-alerts` - Automation: `automation-http-automation` - Utilization: `asset-utilization-http-utilization`, `utilization-data-weekly-http-utilization` - Maintenance: `maintenance-value-http-maintenance` - All data: `all-http-all-data` Search fields (multiple can be specified with OR logic): - `NAME`: Device name - `SERIAL`: Device serial number - `THIRD_PARTY_ID`: Third party identifier - `MAC`: MAC address - `IMEI`: IMEI number - `MANUFACTURER_SERIAL`: Manufacturer serial number Match types: - `EXACT`: Case-insensitive exact match - `CONTAINS`: Case-insensitive contains match Request body filters: - `deviceCategories`: Filter by device category (TRACKER, INFRA, SENSOR) - `orgId`: Organization ID to scope the search (for partner/system users) Query parameters for data inclusion (all default to false for minimal response): - `includeCustomData`: Include custom data fields - `includeSubscriptionInfo`: Include subscription information - `includeProfileInfo`: Include profile information - `includeLocationData`: Include location data - `includeExternalSensorData`: Include external sensor data - `includeOrientationStateData`: Include orientation state data - `includeShockEventData`: Include shock event data - `includeAllData`: Include all data - `fields`: Comma-separated list of field paths to include in response Returns a maximum of 10 devices. Requires DEVICE_METADATA_VIEW permission. ## 5.6.21 Release date: December 03, 2025 ** Geozone ** - Added isPrivacyZone to GET/PUT /rest/area/{id} to enable privacy filtering for geozones - Added allowedLocationStates to GET/PUT /rest/area/{id} to configure which location states are allowed to have coordinates in privacy zones - Privacy zones can now filter location coordinates based on location state (e.g., allow only PERIODIC/SCHEDULED locations, remove ON_THE_MOVE coordinates) ** Location ** - Added originalLocation to AggregatedLocation to store original coordinates before privacy filtering or discard - Added modificationReason to AggregatedLocation to indicate why location coordinates were modified: - NONE: Location coordinates were not modified - PRIVACY_FILTERED: Location coordinates were nullified due to privacy zone filtering rules - DISCARDED_WIFI_NETWORK_FALLBACK: WiFi location was discarded and network location was used as fallback - DISCARDED_WIFI_NO_NETWORK_FALLBACK: WiFi location was discarded but no network fallback was available - WIFI_RESOLUTION_FAILED_NETWORK_FALLBACK: WiFi resolution failed but network fallback was available - WIFI_RESOLUTION_FAILED: WiFi resolution failed and no network fallback was available ## 5.6.20 Release date: November 03, 2025 ** Image Library ** - Added GET /api/v2/image_library/byName/{name} endpoint to retrieve image metadata by name. Uses hierarchical lookup: searches in organization's own images first, then partner's shared images, then system's shared images. Only returns images accessible by the organization. Requires imageType query parameter (ASSET_IMAGE, ASSET_ICON, GEOZONE_IMAGE, GEOZONE_ICON, ORIENTATION, SENSOR). - Added optional query parameter `onlyOwnedByOrg` (default=false) to GET /api/v2/image_library endpoint. When set to true, returns only images owned by the organization. When false, returns all accessible images including those shared from parent organizations. - Deprecated GET /api/v2/image_library/managed endpoint. Use GET /api/v2/image_library?onlyOwnedByOrg=true instead for the same functionality. ## 5.6.19 Release date: October 10, 2025 ** Device ** - Added PUT /API/v2/devices/{serial}/delete_history_data endpoint for deleting historical data with two deletion modes: - **INITIAL_STATE**: Complete reset that brings the tracker back to clean initial state (ignores all other parameters) - **SELECTIVE**: Granular control over data deletion before a specified date using parameters: - `deleteLocationData`: Remove GPS coordinates, geofences, and journey data - `deleteSensorData`: Remove temperature, humidity, and pressure readings - `deleteOrientationData`: Remove accelerometer and movement detection data - `deleteCustomData`: Remove application-specific custom data - `deleteAllData`: Remove all historical data types (overrides individual flags) - `beforeDate`: ISO 8601 date before which to delete data (only used in SELECTIVE mode) - Requires DEVICE_HIST_DATA_DELETE permission. Battery-related data is preserved for recalculation purposes. ** Organisation ** - Updated organization access control for GET /API/v2/organisations and GET /API/v2/organisations/{id} endpoints to support all organization types: - **NORMAL organizations**: Can now access these endpoints but only see their own organization data - **PARTNER organizations**: Continue to see all organizations within their partner group (requires int_api_partner_org feature) - Removed int_api_partner_org feature requirement for NORMAL organizations while maintaining existing access controls ** Geozone ** - Added optional URL parameter `onlyOwnedByOrg` (default=false) to GET /API/v2/geozones endpoint (Get all geozones). If true, returns only geozones directly owned by the organization. If false, also returns geozones made accessible from a higher level through sharing permissions. - Added geozone sharing fields to create and update geozone external API endpoints (POST /API/v2/geozones and PUT /API/v2/geozones/{id}): - `accessibleByOrganizations`: List of organization IDs that should have access to this geozone - `excludedOrganizations`: List of organization IDs that should be excluded from accessing this geozone - `visibleForChildOrgs`: Whether this geozone is visible to child organizations These fields are also returned in geozone responses to show current sharing settings. ## 5.6.18 Release date: September 8, 2025 ** Organisation Diagnostics ** - Added new API endpoint GET /API/v2/organisation/diagnostics for retrieving diagnostic time series data for an organisation within a specific time window. This endpoint provides access to diagnostic overview metrics including device counts, battery status, connectivity metrics, and other organizational health indicators over time. Parameters include optional organisationId (defaults to API key organisation when not provided), from date (supports ISO-8601 format and relative values like 'last_hour', 'last_day', 'last_week'), to date (optional, defaults to now), and diagnosticsName (optional set of strings to filter specific diagnostic types, returns all if empty). Maximum time window is 90 days. Returns structured time series data with metric types mapped to timestamps and their corresponding values. ## 5.6.16 Release date: July 4, 2025 ** Device ** - Added optional `fields` query parameter to device endpoints for field filtering, significantly reducing payload size for clients that only need specific fields. This parameter accepts a comma-separated list of JSON field paths to include in the response. Supported endpoints: - GET /API/v2/devices - List all devices - GET /API/v2/devices/{serial} - Get single device - GET /API/v2/devices/byTags/{tag} - Get devices by tag - POST /API/v2/devices/byFilter - Get devices by filter - Examples: - `?fields=serial,name` - Returns only serial and name fields - `?fields=serial,ownedByOrg.name,subscriptionInfo.endDate` - Returns serial, organization name, and subscription end date - `?fields=serial,currentLat,currentLng,batteryInfo.percentageRemaining` - Returns location and battery data only - Supports nested field paths using dot notation (e.g., `ownedByOrg.name`, `subscriptionInfo.endDate`, `batteryInfo.percentageRemaining`) - When not specified, all fields are returned (default behavior preserved) - Field filtering is applied AFTER all existing authorization and feature filters (@SuppressInDevAPI, @ExposeInDevAPI), ensuring proper access control is maintained - This feature is particularly useful for mobile clients, dashboard widgets, or integrations that only need a subset of device data - Added pagination support to GET /API/v2/devices endpoint with optional `limit` and `offset` query parameters: - `limit` (optional): Maximum number of devices to return. If not specified, all devices are returned. - `offset` (optional): Number of devices to skip before starting to return results. Default is 0. - Examples: - `?limit=50` - Returns first 50 devices - `?limit=100&offset=50` - Returns devices 51-150 - `?limit=20&offset=0&fields=serial,name` - Returns first 20 devices with only serial and name fields - Pagination works seamlessly with field filtering and all other query parameters - The internal implementation continues to use efficient keyset pagination (100 devices per internal page) to manage transaction sizes while respecting the external limit/offset parameters ** Image Library ** - Added comprehensive Image Library External API v2 endpoints for managing images: - GET /api/v2/image_library - List all accessible images with optional filtering by image type - GET /api/v2/image_library/managed - Returns list of images which are managed by the organization - GET /api/v2/image_library/{id} - Get metadata for a specific image - GET /api/v2/image_library/download/{id} - Download image file with optional size parameter (thumbnail, medium, large, original) - GET /api/v2/image_library/base64/{id} - Get image as base64 encoded string for embedding - POST /api/v2/image_library - Upload new image with multipart form data (file, name, imageType, keywords, visibility) - PUT /api/v2/image_library/{id} - Update image metadata (name, keywords, visibility settings) - DELETE /api/v2/image_library/{id} - Delete an image from the library All endpoints require 'image_library' feature and valid API key authentication. Images support various types: ASSET_IMAGE, ASSET, ASSET_PHOTO, GEOZONE_IMAGE, GEOZONE_ICON, ORIENTATION, SENSOR, CONFIGURATION_ICON. Visibility can be controlled at organization, partner, and child organization levels. ** Device Diagnostics ** - Added new API endpoint POST /API/v2/devices/{serial}/diagnostics/analyzewindow for computing diagnostic analysis within a specified time window. This endpoint provides real-time diagnostic calculations for a specific device and time range, complementing the existing snapshot-based diagnostics API. The endpoint accepts fromDate and toDate parameters in the request body and returns detailed analysis results. - Enhanced POST /API/v2/devices/diagnostics/data endpoint to support retrieving the latest completed snapshot when no snapshot IDs are provided. Previously, snapshot IDs were required. Now, if the request body is empty or contains an empty array, the endpoint will automatically return data from the most recent completed diagnostic snapshot. - Added optional orgId query parameter to POST /API/v2/devices/diagnostics/data endpoint. When provided along with an empty snapshot IDs list, the endpoint retrieves the latest completed snapshot for the specified organization instead of the API key's default organization. ** Device ** - Added diagnosticWarnings field to GET /API/v2/devices response. This field contains an array of current diagnostic warning objects for each device, with each warning including the metric name, threshold value that was violated, and warning type (minor or major). This provides direct access to diagnostic status information that was previously only available in the device detail view. - Added deliveryNoteNumber field to GET /API/v2/devices and GET /API/v2/device/{serial} responses. This field contains the delivery note number for tracking purposes and is only accessible to System organization users. The field supports search, filtering, sorting, and bulk operations through the platform interface. ** Reports ** - Added optional deliveryMethod query parameter to POST /API/v2/reports/custom endpoint. This parameter accepts ReportDeliveryMethod enum values (ATTACHMENT or LINK) to specify whether the Excel report should be delivered as an email attachment or as a download link. Defaults to LINK when not specified. This provides the same functionality available in scheduled reports for on-demand custom report uploads. ** Organisation ** - Added optional nameFilter query parameter to GET /API/v2/organisations endpoint. This parameter allows filtering organizations by name using case-insensitive partial matching. When provided, the API returns only organizations whose names contain the specified filter string. ** Dashboard ** - Added new API endpoint GET /API/v2/dashboard/{dashboardId} for retrieving a specific dashboard by ID. This endpoint provides detailed dashboard information including configuration, type-specific properties, and metadata. Requires 'dashboard' role permission and validates access to the dashboard's owning organization. ## 5.6.15 Release date: June 2, 2025 - extended the organisation object with additional statistics - added allowNetworkLocationResolution and maxNetworkLocationRadiusForResolution to the geozone APIs call. ## 5.6.14 Release date: April 30, 2025 - added geobeaconSerial to the result of the /device/$serial call. Only relevant for device which act as a geobeacon

get.summary: Get the GPS locations of a specific tracking device
get.description:
This method allows to retrieve the tracked GPS locations of a specific tracking device ordered by time (newest first). Each tracked location contains the latitude, longitude, timestamp (ISO-8601 format) and state (START, STOP, ON THE MOVE). If latitude and longitude are both zero this means no GPS fix could be made.

---

**Security Requirements:**

**Required Permissions** (at least one):

- View device historic data (locations, journey, activity)

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: From date. Use "all" to retrieve all data. Other possible values are: last_minute, last_hour, last_day, last_week. Specific dates are formatted in ISO-8601 format ('yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ', e.g. '2014-07-11T12:08:00+0200')

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: To date formatted in ISO-8601 ('yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ', e.g. '2014-07-11T12:08:00+0200'). If not specified, current datetime is taken as to date.

post.summary: Get paged geozone visits by filter
post.description:
Get paged geozone visits across all devices in the organization, optionally filtered by device and geozone criteria. Returns visits that overlap with the specified time window (from/to). A visit is included if its entry time is on or before the 'to' date AND its exit time is after the 'from' date (or the visit is still ongoing). This means long-running visits that started before the 'from' date are still returned if they haven't ended or ended after 'from'. Max time window is 366 days. Results are ordered by device serial ascending, then entry time ascending. Use startIndex and maxResults for pagination; the response 'count' field gives the total number of matching visits.

---

**Security Requirements:**

**Required Permissions** (at least one):

- View geozone visits

**AND**

**Required Features** (at least one):

- Geozone visits report

requestBody.description: Geozone visit query with optional device and geozone filters. The 'from' and 'to' fields are required ISO-8601 timestamps. deviceFilter and geozoneFilter are optional and use the same format as POST /devices/byFilter and POST /geozones/byFilter respectively.

Basic query (no filters).description: Get all geozone visits in a time window with pagination

Combined device and geozone filters.description: Get geozone visits filtered by both device asset tags and geozone tags

Paged geozone visit response.description: A paged geozone visit response with count, data array and success flag.

400.description: Invalid request: bad date format, time window exceeds 366 days, or invalid filter parameters

401.description: Unauthorized access. No API key provided or API key invalid

403.description: Missing required feature (geozone_visit_report) or permission (GEOZONE_VISIT_VIEW)

status.description:
The status of the tracker
* ONLINE : 'The tracker recently has communicated, see also last seen alive'
* OFFLINE : 'The tracker has not communicated for 2 days or, if present, 3 times the keepalive period'
* THROTTLING : 'The tracker has exceeded the available message budget and can only send a limited amount of messages'
* READY_FOR_USE : 'The tracker has a subscription and is ready to communicate, but has not communicated yet'
* OFF_CONTRACT : 'The tracker has a subscription that is expired or terminated'
* NOT_ACTIVATED : 'The tracker does not have a subscription or the contract has not started yet'
* ACTIVATION_IN_PROGRESS : 'The tracker has a subscription, but is waiting for the communication provider to become ready for use'
* ACTIVATION_ERROR : 'An error has occurred with the communication provider'
